title: Prevent starting and stopping legacy hyperv machines when not elevated
body:
This follows the ongoing work on enhancing Hyper-v UX.
It adds a check to verify user has elevated rights when working with old podman machines (podman < 6). This is to prevent possible misbehavior.

It resolves #27615 

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [x] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed) 
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [ ] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
When attempting to stop/start Hyper-V machines created with Podman 5.x or earlier from a non-administrative terminal, the system now displays a clear error suggesting the command be run as an administrator.
```

comments.body: @l0rd another one ... i did not add any tests as i'm waiting for https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27597. If it's close to be done i can mark this as draft and complete it later.
id prefer you mark it as draft so i people dont review it until you are ready for a review ?

> id prefer you mark it as draft so i people dont review it until you are ready for a review ?

the code logic is done and ready to be reviewed. I was referring to missing e2e tests. There is another PR to be merged before adding them otherwise they won't work. CI needs to run as normal user.
Converting it as draft for the moment but feel free to give feedback

> @lstocchi I won't be able to fix #27597 right away. My brute force attempts to run tests as a normal user in the CI VM (windows server) have failed. I am trying to provision the VM on AWS to debug what's wrong but it will take time. And, even if we solve that, we still need to discuss if we can switch between users that have different roles (admin/hyvervadmin/unprivileged) in the same test. My goal for now is to run the WSL and installation tests as a normal user.
> 
> Apart from that we may still add tests to verify the start and stop of a legacy VM (created with a 5.7 client) but I would do that in a separate PR.
> 
> I looked at the code and did some tests locally and LGTM. I have added the label `No New Tests` to trigger the CI: a couple of tests are failing, but they look flakes, I will keep an eye on them.

Ok, marked as ready then. 

For the user switch i tried to execute a command as a normal user from an elevated shell by duplicating the token of the explore.exe process (which should run as a normal user by default) and it worked. I don't think you can do the opposite (normal -> elevated) without any UAC interaction. We need to do some test. BTW dropping the sample code here fyi https://gist.github.com/lstocchi/9404a031243bed97b7a85ca64f982a9e
